title: Why Americans Are Sicker Than Ever w/ Alex Clark

chunks.title: Introduction to Health Issues
chunks.summary: The podcast begins with a discussion about health as a bipartisan issue, highlighting a recent Senate hearing involving significant figures advocating for health reform. The conversation segues into personal health journeys and mutual values among millennials regarding health and relationships.
chunks.transcript: on this week's episode of the podcast I talked to Alex Clark she is a conservative podcaster her podcast is called culture Apothecary but I've never really talked about politics before on my podcast this isn't meant to be a political podcast the issue of health and the issue of making America healthy again has become a bipartisan issue and that is the intent that's my really talked about in this podcast which is what it's like to be a millennial woman dating and what men who are millennials or gen xers or gen zers might be looking for in a woman and maybe how to ourselves but then very quickly about who we choose as a partner and how we align those values so we definitely talked about that with Alex in this episode as well please enjoy this podcast with Alex Clark I

chunks.title: Personal Health Transformation
chunks.summary: Alex shares her personal journey of health transformation triggered by her awareness of the opioid crisis and the corrupt practices of big food and pharma. She discusses how this led her to question previously accepted norms, including hormonal birth control and processed food.

chunks.title: The Senate Hearing Experience
chunks.summary: Alex describes her experience at the Senate hearing, emphasizing the importance of the discussions around food and pharmaceutical corruption. She mentions the need for accountability and voices of millennials, particularly women, in advocating for health reforms.

chunks.title: Challenges of Modern Parenting
chunks.summary: The conversation touches on the challenges of raising healthy children in a society saturated with unhealthy food options. There is a focus on how modern parents, particularly moms, feel overwhelmed and misled by institutions regarding food safety.

chunks.title: Health and Diet Choices
chunks.summary: Alex discusses the misconceptions surrounding diet, particularly concerning processed foods and hormonal treatments. She shares insights into how societal norms and dietary guidelines have failed women, leading to health crises.
chunks.transcript: Senator Ron Johnson who is a conservative was hosting it and he brought RFK Jr who's not a conservative um and and all these different people from different walks of life together and it was a totally nonpartisan panel there were some people on there that were Democrats there were some people who were Republicans none of that matter none of it even got brought up I have no idea what everyone was we weren't you know talking about it behind the scenes the point was who is willing to have this discussion who's willing to get together um and you know historically conservatives our position has always been it's personal responsibility it's up to the person if if they want to eat junk if they're if they want to eat healthy good that's their choice and my point and what I've been trying to talk about on my show is that you can't ask people to have uh to take personal responsibility and make healthier your decisions when the entire system is rigged and everything is is kind of leveraged against us whether it's marketing whether it's Google searches you know what big big Tech um I I one of my friends and I were talking about this recently one of my guests my podcast is that remember during the pandemic talking about getting sunlight was shadowbanned that was shadowbanned so don't tell us that we need to take more personal responsibility and make better decisions when you're blocking information on freaking sunlight it's insane and most of my audience knows that I got canceled during the pandemic so my my Instagram was deleted in November of 2021 and I had to man you built back we had to restart from zero I guess people find it valuable yeah so in November 2021 got deleted my lur agents were just ringing their hands and they were why do you keep talking about this and I just couldn't stop talking about it but the pandemic is just a crazy

chunks.title: Dating and Relationships
chunks.summary: The podcast shifts to advice about dating and relationships, discussing the dynamics of attraction and the importance of men understanding women's cycles. Alex advises men to be proactive in taking the lead and suggests that understanding women's health is crucial for successful dating.

chunks.title: Value in Relationships
chunks.summary: Discussion around what women find attractive in men revolves around qualities such as leadership, health awareness, and traditional values. The podcast emphasizes the importance of being informed and confident in one's role as a partner.

chunks.title: Public Interaction and Approaching Women
chunks.summary: They explore the nuances of how men should approach women in public, including the importance of eye contact and body language. The conversation highlights that men should take the initiative to open dialogues in natural settings.

chunks.title: Health Conspiracies and Criticism
chunks.summary: Alex discusses the ongoing criticism surrounding health norms, including public health policies and the misunderstanding of dietary influences on health. They touch upon the societal acceptance of unhealthy food and the perils of misinformation in mainstream health discussions.
chunks.transcript: are people on the on the left having their eyes open yeah I mean RFK Jr is one of those people and so to me when I see somebody like that say okay here here's what I think is interesting this is something that RFK Jr has been fighting for his entire life right he easily could have said no no I want nothing to do with Trump I want nothing to do with the Republican Party um I don't care like this is where this stops and my activism my advocacy like it stops here the cause is so important to him that he said I'm going to compromise on this and I'm going to band together with these people who I don't agree with on everything to get this Mission done whereas I see people and I will call out Senator Corey Booker why is Corey Booker who had been advocating for real food for years refusing to endorse Trump why is he not saying look I absolutely can't stand Trump I hate everything else but if this is who's willing to get this done this supposed mission that I have cared about for how many years that I've been saying we need this we need to get you know organic food to more children not OIC why would you not put that aside to work on this so then I think who actually cares about this if you cannot ban with people who you were told allegedly were the bad guys if they're willing to to um you know work towards the same Mission as you then how important is that mission to you it's crazy and it's just I don't know like what a crazy time you know that that we can't all just agree that like health is Paramount like why are we not prioritizing this why does it have to even be a political issue it's just it's an insane thing to me when when you okay America right now is the Titanic we are sinking um you are not going to be asking each person we should not be asking each person drowning in the ocean are you a Republican or Democrat before we hand you a life jacket or send a life ref to you right um right now Health in America we we are the sinking Titanic

chunks.title: The Importance of Traditional Values
chunks.summary: The episode concludes on a note reflecting on the significance of traditional values in modern life, from family roles to societal expectations. The discussion emphasizes the need for awareness and a return to foundational cultural norms regarding family and health.
